title: System and method to facilitate cross-layer optimization of video over wifi in an information-centric networking environment
abstract: A method is provided in one example embodiment and may include determining each station of a plurality of stations connected to a WiFi access point that are to receive downlink video transmissions from the WiFi access point, wherein the WiFi access point is connected to an Information-Centric Networking-based (ICN-based) network; associating each station of the plurality of stations to each of a corresponding ICN face of a plurality of ICN faces maintained by the WiFi access point; and managing a downlink transmission buffer for each station of the plurality of stations based, at least in part, a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) associated with downlink video transmissions for each station of the plurality of stations, wherein each downlink transmission buffer is associated with the corresponding ICN face associated with each station.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIM ED IS: 1. A method comprising: determining each station of a plurality of stations connected to a WiFi access point that are to receive downlink video transmissions from the WiFi access point, wherein the WiFi access point is connected to an Information-Centric Networking-based (ICN-based) network; associating each station of the plurality of stations to each of a corresponding ICN face of a plurality of ICN faces maintained by the WiFi access point; and managing a downlink transmission buffer for each station of the plurality of stations based, at least in part, a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) associated with downlink video transmissions for each station of the plurality of stations, wherein each downlink transmission buffer is associated with the corresponding ICN face associated with each station. 2. The method of Claim 1, wherein the managing further comprises: (a) determining a total available bandwidth of a channel to provide downlink video transmissions to the plurality of stations; (b) identifying an optimized video rate for each station that maximizes utilization of the channel for the plurality of stations while providing fairness of downlink transmissions toward each station; (c) enqueuing downlink video packets to each downlink transmission buffer for each station of the plurality of stations; and (d) scheduling downlink transmissions from each downlink transmission buffer for each station of the plurality of stations based on the identified optimized video rate for each station. 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein the optimized video rate identified for each station is less than or equal to a maximum physical data rate associated with the lowest MCS assigned to downlink transmissions for the station that maximizes utilization of the channel for the plurality of stations while providing a fair utilization of air time of the channel for the plurality of stations. 4. The method of Claim 2 or 3, further comprising at least one of: evaluating the MCS associated with downlink transmissions for each station of the plurality of stations at periodic intervals; and determining that an interest message for a particular video content has not been received from a particular station within a threshold time period. 5. The method of Claim 4, further comprising: repeating (a), (b), and (c) at a period interval or upon determining that an interest message for a particular video content has not been received from a particular station within a threshold time period. 6. The method of any of Claims 1 to 5, wherein the ICN-based network is one of: a content-centric network; a named data network; and an Internet Protocol (IP) network provisioned with at least one hybrid ICN-enabled routing node. 7. The method of Claim 6, wherein the at least one hybrid ICN-enabled routing node is capable of performing packet forwarding using both IP networking constructs and ICN constructs. 8. One or more non-transitory tangible media encoding logic that includes instructions for execution by a processor, wherein the execution causes the processor to perform operations comprising: determining each station of a plurality of stations connected to a WiFi access point that are to receive downlink video transmissions from the WiFi access point, wherein the WiFi access point is connected to an Information-Centric Networking-based (ICN-based) network; associating each station of the plurality of stations to each of a corresponding ICN face of a plurality of ICN faces maintained by the WiFi access point; and managing a downlink transmission buffer for each station of the plurality of stations based, at least in part, a modulation and coding scheme (MCS) associated with downlink video 

 transmissions for each station of the plurality of stations, wherein each downlink transmission buffer is associated with the corresponding ICN face associated with each station. 9. The media of Claim 9 or 10 7 wherein the execution causes the processor to perform further operations comprising: evaluating the MCS associated with downlink transmissions for each station of the plurality of stations at periodic intervals; and determining that an interest message for a particular video content has not been received from a particular station within a threshold time period. 12.  repeating (a), (b), and (c) at a periodic interval or upon determining that an interest message for a particular video content has not been received from a particular station within the threshold time period. 13. The apparatus according to claim 21 further comprising means for implementing the method according to any of claims 2 to 7. 23. A computer program, computer program product or computer readable medium comprising instructions which, when executed by a computer, cause the computer to carry out the steps of the method of any of claims 1 to 7.
